text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon, in the quaint town of Willow Creek, lived an old owl named Orion. Orion was notorious not just for his wisdom but for his peculiar preferences. He had a favorite crinkled newspaper that smelled faintly of maple leaves. Every weekday morning, he'd perch on a low branch near the town library, devouring last night's weather forecast. This habit was a mystery even to himself, yet it gave him endless entertainment.

One drearily cloudy day, Orion decided he'd had enough reading material. He swooped down from his resting spot and fluttered about, scanning the skies for something interesting. That's when he saw it: a flock of pelicans sailing south. Normally, he'd remain snug in his nest all day, unless something piqued his interest, such. He fluttered back to his perch and turned the newspaper on its edge.

As the pelicans passed by, their graceful curves turning the sky to a patchwork pattern of blues and whites
